current.summary: Completed full develop workflow (brainstorm→plan→validate→beads) for Tier 3 hook adapters: 6 providers researched, 4 adapters designed, implementation plan written and validated

activeWork.description: Tier 3 hook adapter implementation — 4 new adapters + 6 provider research docs
activeWork.progress: All pre-execution work complete: design doc (multi-persona reviewed), implementation plan (1035 lines, 47 tasks, quality-reviewed, validated), 7 phase beads with dependency chain, Phase B deep analysis done. Ready for Phase 0 execution.

recentDecisions: Pure-Go TS parsing: esbuild (TS→JS strip) + go-fAST (JS→AST) — avoids CGO entirely, no build pipeline changes
Adapter order: Windsurf > VS Code Copilot > Factory Droid > Pi (highest user impact first)
Pi adapter uses structured marker comments for lossless round-trip + heuristic AST decode for hand-written extensions
Security: json.Marshal() for all TS interpolation, fail-closed blocking, marker/code cross-validation
OpenCode = SST's anomalyco/opencode (133K stars, TypeScript), NOT the archived Go project that became Crush
Crush (Charm) has NO hooks — docs only, no adapter needed
Factory Droid is CC-identical for hooks (9 events, same JSON schema)
Provider research docs require Sonnet validation loops until zero issues
nextSteps: Start execution: claim syllago-18rcp and run Phase 0 (toolmap updates + VerifyFields extension)
Phase 1: Windsurf adapter (split-event encode/decode) — the most architecturally interesting adapter
Phase 2-3: VS Code Copilot + Factory Droid adapters (straightforward CC variants)
Phase 4: Pi adapter (esbuild + go-fAST TS parsing, template codegen, security mitigations)
Phase 5: Provider research docs for 6 providers with Sonnet validation loops
Phase 6: Final validation (build, test, coverage, registry completeness)

context.notes: 277 uncommitted files are pre-existing (content library, TUI work, etc.) — not from this session. This session only produced design/plan docs and develop state files. The tier2-complete-ready-for-tier3 handoff was claimed and its beads (syllago-h5rsk, syllago-ms77k, syllago-pg2cy) were closed as superseded by the new phase beads. Key research findings cached in this session: Pi has 25+ extension events (TypeScript pi.on() pattern), Factory Droid uses CC-identical hook JSON, Crush confirmed no hooks, OpenCode/SST vs OpenCode/Crush split documented. go-fAST (164 stars) chosen over goja (6.8K) for better ES6+ support — verify arrow function parsing works in Phase 4a.

previousSessions.summary: Completed hook adapter Tier 2 migration — all 5 adapters off legacy bridge, shared helpers, degradation enforcement, ADR
completedWork: All 8 Tier 2 phases executed: hookhelpers.go (11 shared helpers), 5 adapter migrations, legacy bridge removal, Verify() upgrade
Kitchen sink test fixtures moved to testdata/
nextSteps: VS Code Copilot adapter, Windsurf split-event adapter
